reasoning: The document has high fidelity metrics but automated programmatic detectors identified 4 major structural and accessibility issues. These include lists broken by page breaks, incorrect list marker conversions, an incomplete image alt text, and a full-page image extracted alongside text. Since there are 4 major defects, count-based routing rules require HUMAN_REVIEW.

concerns.description: Full-page image alongside extracted text (1 page(s))
concerns.analysis: suspect_pages=1. A scanned/screenshot page image duplicates content already present as text elements.

concerns.description: List broken by page break (2 instance(s)): p2→p3: list item ends “…arding air pollution. Comments were made”, continues as paragraph; p3→p4: list item ends “…,000 people are relying on water intakes”, continues as paragraph
concerns.analysis: split_list_count=2. Lists that span page breaks should be extracted as a single continuous structure. Split lists affect reading order and navigability for assistive technology.

concerns.description: Lists converted incorrectly: 2 ordered list(s) rendered as alphabetical (A, B, C) but contain roman numeral markers (I, II, III) — e.g. "II. March 24, 2016 Public Meeting and Oral Comments Summary"; "II. Public Meeting Sign-in Forms"
concerns.analysis: roman_letter_confusion=2. The renderer misclassified roman numeral lists as alphabetical starting at letter 'I'. Item text retains original roman numerals alongside incorrect letter markers, breaking list semantics.

concerns.description: Incomplete image alt text: 1 image(s) with inadequate descriptions (1 truncated mid-sentence) — e.g. p1: "...nvironmental Quality memorandum dated May 13, 2016"
concerns.analysis: incomplete_alt_text=1 (truncated=1, placeholder=0, empty=0). Per WCAG 2.1 AA, meaningful images must have descriptive alternative text for screen-reader accessibility.

The word 'MEMORANDUM' on page 1 is prominently underlined in the source PDF. In the output JSON, it is rendered as a level 1 heading but lacks any formatting or HTML tag (such as <u>) to preserve the underline emphasis.
concerns.output_quote: MEMORANDUM (plain text within a heading block)

concerns.description: Degraded image quality: 1 image(s) used fallback rendering instead of direct extraction
concerns.analysis: fallback_rendered_images=1. Examples: p1: "A North Carolina Department of Environmental Quali". These images were not directly extracted from the PDF — instead, an approximate page region was rendered as a PNG. Fallback images may be cropped incorrectly, show the wrong content, or miss meaningful visual elements.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (1): State of North Carolina | Environmental Quality | Energy, Mi
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=1. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.

action_items: Verify and remove duplicate content caused by the full-page image extraction
Merge split lists across page breaks to ensure continuous structure
Correct the list markers from alphabetical to Roman numerals
Update the truncated image alt text with a complete description
